title: uxtextarea
html:
uxtextarea

Add to My Topics

Feedback
  • Last UpdatedApr 29, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

The uxtextarea widget is a multi-line text input control. It allows entry of various formats of multi-line text.

Default Logical Widgets

The default logical widget is ux-TextArea.

Triggers

(web)trigger onchange: This trigger is fired when any of the following actions are true:
  • Value is changed
  • Widget looses focus

Extends fluent-text-area Web Component

This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-text-area. Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-text-area web component.

Keyboard support

  • Text input
  • Vertical navigation keys to scroll the value
  • Horizontal navigation keys to navigate the text-inputted value

Layout processing

Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features.

Widget properties

The uxtextarea widget supports the following properties:
Table 1. Widget Properties
Property Description
html:title Defines a hover over text or tooltip, currently used as helper text.
html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows the textarea.
html:tabindex Allows or prevents a field from getting sequential focus usually with the Tab key.
html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only.
html:placeholder Defines the placeholder text to be shown when no field value is specified.
html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled.
html:resize Defines the enum value that controls the resize behavior for the uxtextarea. The available options are:
  • auto (default)
  • none
  • both
  • horizontal
  • vertical

When set to auto inside uf-layout, resize depends on the selected layout:

  • vertical stretch => horizontal-only resize
  • horizontal stretch => vertical-only resize
  • stretch on both axes => resize disabled
  • no stretch => resize both
html:maxlength Defines the maximum number of input characters allowed.
html:minlength Defines the minimum number of input characters allowed.
html:appearance Defines the appearance of the widget. The available options are:
  • outline (default)
  • filled
Note: It is not promoted by the Property Inspector.
html:cols Defines the width of the widget. The value should be a positive integer. The default value is 20 characters.
html:placeholder Defines a default exemplar value to display whenever the field is empty.
class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class.
label-text Defines the label text.
html:rows Defines the number of visible text lines.
html:spellcheck Defines whether to enable spell checking or not for the <textarea>. If not enabled, the default spell checking configuration is used.
label-position Defines the position of the label with respect to its widget. The available options are:
  • above(default)
  • below
  • before
  • after
Note: If the label-position is not defined for the widget, then it is aligned by default above the widget.
exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid.
Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget.

Helper and validation error visualization

A uxtextarea deals with the following error visualizations:
  • Red color border alert
  • Alert icon (after suffix icon)
  • Tool-tip when hovering the "Alert icon" showing the error message
  • When the uxtextarea has focus, no red border is shown, only error icon is shown
  • When the uxtextarea does not have focus, both red border and error icon is shown

UI blocking

When the field is in transaction, the uxtextarea widget blocks user input by becoming read-only.

Widget styling

CSS Selector Selected node(s)
.u-text-area Widget node
.u-text-area.u-invalid Widget node when the value is invalid
.u-text-area.readonly or .u-text-area[readonly] Widget node when the widget is read-only
.u-text-area.disabled or .u-text-area[disabled] Widget node when the widget is disabled
.u-text-area:hover Widget node when the widget is hovered
.u-text-area:active Widget node when the widget is active
.u-text-area:focus Widget node when the widget has focus
.u-text-area:focus-within Widget node when the widget has focus-within
.u-text-area:focus-visible Widget node when the widget has focus-visible
.u-text-area::part(root) Root node
.u-text-area::part(label) Label node
.u-text-area::part(control) Control node
.u-text-area::part(end) End node
.u-text-area .u-label-text Label text node
.u-text-area .u-error-icon Error icon node
.u-text-area[u-label-position=<position-value>] Widget node with u-label-position=<position-value>. The available values for position-value are:
  • above
  • below
  • before
  • after
.u-stretchable Defines how the widget stretches, allowing it to resize dynamically based on the layout rules.

Widget sources

The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/text_area.

Related Links
  • ux-TextArea

    This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-TextArea properties.

  • UX Widgets

    Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder.
